annotations.label: Square roots via Euler's criterion

anchor.snippet: using Euler's criterion one can give an explicit formula for the

annotations.note: Euler's criterion (IsSquare ↔ a^(p/2)=1) is formalized as ZMod.euler_criterion, but the explicit square-root formula a^((p+1)/4) for p≡3 mod 4 is not.

annotations.label: Law of quadratic reciprocity

anchor.value: Law of quadratic reciprocity — Let p and

annotations.note: legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity states (q/p)·(p/q)=(-1)^((p-1)(q-1)/4) for distinct odd primes.

annotations.label: Prime divisors of n²−5

anchor.snippet: Therefore, except for

annotations.note: This is an illustrative motivating example about which primes divide n²−5; no such statement exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadratic residue

anchor.snippet: is a quadratic residue whenever it is congruent to a square

annotations.note: The notion is expressed via IsSquare (a : ZMod p) (e.g. legendreSym.eq_one_iff), but there is no standalone `quadraticResidue` definition.

annotations.label: Multiplicative properties of residues

anchor.snippet: There are an equal number of quadratic residues and non-residues

annotations.note: quadraticChar_sum_zero (sum of the quadratic character is 0) encodes the equal count of residues and non-residues, and quadraticChar's multiplicativity gives the product rules, but no single card-equality decl states it.

annotations.label: Failure for composite modulus 15

anchor.snippet: there are only 3 quadratic residues (1, 4 and 9) in the multiplicative group modulo 15

annotations.note: A specific numeric illustration mod 15; not present as a statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetric entries (Legendre)

anchor.snippet: are symmetric around the diagonal

annotations.note: Symmetry of the table when one prime is ≡1 mod 4 is exactly legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity_one_mod_four ((q/p)=(p/q)).

annotations.label: Antisymmetric entries (Legendre)

anchor.snippet: on the other hand, are antisymmetric

annotations.note: Antisymmetry when both primes are ≡3 mod 4 is legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity_three_mod_four ((q/p)=-(p/q)).

annotations.label: First Supplement

anchor.snippet: is congruent to 1 modulo 4

annotations.note: ZMod.exists_sq_eq_neg_one_iff says -1 is a square mod p iff p%4≠3, with legendreSym.at_neg_one giving (-1/p)=χ₄ p.

annotations.label: Second Supplement

anchor.snippet: is congruent to ±1 modulo 8

annotations.note: ZMod.exists_sq_eq_two_iff says 2 is a square mod p iff p%8∈{1,7}, with legendreSym.at_two giving (2/p)=χ₈ p.

annotations.label: Character of −3

anchor.snippet: −3 is a quadratic residue modulo every prime which is a residue modulo 3

annotations.note: No exists_sq_eq lemma for ±3 (only ±1 and ±2) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Character of 5

anchor.snippet: 5 is a quadratic residue modulo every prime which is a residue modulo 5

annotations.note: No exists_sq_eq lemma characterizing when 5 is a residue exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Higher q observations

anchor.snippet: The observations about −3 and 5 continue to hold

annotations.note: These per-value observations are derivable from QR but not stated as Mathlib lemmas.

annotations.label: Character of −5 via CRT

anchor.snippet: either both 5 and −1 have to be residues

annotations.note: A worked CRT example for -5; not present as a statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chinese Remainder Theorem

anchor.snippet: Using the Chinese remainder theorem

annotations.note: ZMod.chineseRemainder gives the ring isomorphism ZMod(mn) ≃+* ZMod m × ZMod n for coprime m,n, used to combine the mod 4 and mod 5 congruences here.

annotations.label: Quadratic Reciprocity (Gauss's statement)

anchor.snippet: Gauss's statement

annotations.note: Gauss's residue/non-residue statement is the same content as legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity.

annotations.label: Quadratic Reciprocity (combined statement)

anchor.snippet: combined statement

annotations.note: The combined sign statement is exactly legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity.

annotations.label: Quadratic Reciprocity (Legendre's statement)

anchor.snippet: Legendre's statement

annotations.note: Legendre's (p/q)(q/p)=(-1)^((p-1)/2·(q-1)/2) is the verbatim form of legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity.

annotations.label: Legendre symbol of −1 via Euler's criterion

anchor.snippet: follows directly from Euler's criterion

annotations.note: legendreSym.at_neg_one gives (-1/p)=χ₄ p, the first-supplement value derived (via legendreSym.eq_pow) from Euler's criterion.

annotations.label: Fermat's theorems on quadratic forms

anchor.snippet: a number of theorems about expressing a prime by a quadratic form

annotations.note: The sum-of-two-squares case (p=x²+y² for p%4≠3) is Nat.Prime.sq_add_sq, but the x²+2y² and x²+3y² representation theorems are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fermat's claim for primes ending 7 and 3

anchor.snippet: if the prime number p ends with 7

annotations.note: Fermat's claims about p=x²+5y² (primes ≡3,7 mod 20 etc.) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler–Lagrange result

anchor.snippet: Euler conjectured, and Lagrange proved

annotations.note: The Euler/Lagrange results on representation by x²+5y² are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler's statement of reciprocity

anchor.snippet: for distinct odd primes p and q

annotations.note: Euler's periodicity formulation (the symbol depends only on p mod 4a) is captured by jacobiSym.mod_right, but his exact ±-prime statement is not a single named theorem.

annotations.label: Fermat's little theorem

anchor.snippet: if p is a prime number and a is an integer

annotations.note: ZMod.pow_card_sub_one_eq_one states a^(p-1)=1 in ZMod p for a≠0, the form used here.

annotations.label: Legendre symbol

anchor.snippet: This is now known as the Legendre symbol

annotations.note: legendreSym p a is defined as quadraticChar (ZMod p) a, giving 0/1/-1 exactly as the Legendre symbol.

annotations.label: Legendre's version of quadratic reciprocity

anchor.snippet: He notes that these can be combined

annotations.note: Legendre's combined version is the content of legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity.

annotations.label: Third law for character −2

anchor.section: The supplementary laws using Legendre symbols
anchor.snippet: either -1 or 2 are both quadratic residues, or both non-residues

annotations.note: ZMod.exists_sq_eq_neg_two_iff says -2 is a square mod p iff p%8∈{1,3}, with legendreSym.at_neg_two giving (-2/p)=χ₈' p.

annotations.label: Legendre's Theorem

anchor.section: The supplementary laws using Legendre symbols
anchor.snippet: Let a , b and c be integers where any pair of the three are relatively prime

annotations.note: Legendre's theorem on nontrivial solubility of ax²+by²+cz²=0 (Legendre/Hasse–Minkowski for ternary forms) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Proof of Theorem I

anchor.section: The supplementary laws using Legendre symbols
anchor.snippet: Theorem I is handled by letting

annotations.note: Legendre's attempted derivation of a QR case from his ternary-form theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Legendre's Lemma

anchor.section: The supplementary laws using Legendre symbols
anchor.snippet: If p is a prime that is congruent to 1 modulo 4 then there exists an odd prime q

annotations.note: This auxiliary existence-of-auxiliary-prime lemma (a Dirichlet-type input to Legendre's proof) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gauss's general statement

anchor.snippet: If p is a prime of the form 4 n + 1 then p

annotations.note: Gauss's general residue/non-residue statement (with the 4n+1 case = quadratic_reciprocity_one_mod_four) is the content of legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity.

annotations.label: Gauss's Lemma

anchor.snippet: If p is a prime congruent to 1 modulo 8 then there exists an odd prime q

annotations.note: This is the auxiliary existence lemma from Gauss's first (inductive) proof, not the classical counting Gauss's lemma (ZMod.gauss_lemma); the existence lemma is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gauss's Version in Legendre Symbols

anchor.snippet: Gauss's Version in Legendre Symbols

annotations.note: Gauss's Legendre-symbol version restates legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity.

annotations.label: Gauss's Combined Version in Legendre Symbols

anchor.snippet: Gauss's Combined Version in Legendre Symbols

annotations.note: The combined Legendre-symbol version is exactly legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity.

annotations.label: Euler's Formulation of Quadratic Reciprocity

anchor.snippet: Euler's Formulation of Quadratic Reciprocity

annotations.note: Euler's formulation (value of the symbol depends only on the residue mod 4a) is captured by jacobiSym.mod_right, but his precise ±-condition statement is not a dedicated theorem.

annotations.label: Quadratic Reciprocity (Gauss; Fourth Proof)

anchor.snippet: Let a , b , c , ... be unequal positive odd primes, whose product is n

annotations.note: The product-over-primes formulation is the Jacobi-symbol reciprocity law jacobiSym.quadratic_reciprocity.

annotations.label: Gauss's fourth proof example

anchor.snippet: Let a = 3, b = 5, c = 7, and d = 11

annotations.note: A specific numeric illustration of the fourth-proof formulation; not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Eisenstein's Formulation of Quadratic Reciprocity

anchor.snippet: Eisenstein's Formulation of Quadratic Reciprocity

annotations.note: Eisenstein's restatement is logically equivalent to and follows from legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity.

annotations.label: Mordell's Formulation of Quadratic Reciprocity

anchor.snippet: Mordell's Formulation of Quadratic Reciprocity

annotations.note: Mordell's restatement is equivalent to and follows from legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity.

annotations.label: Zeta function formulation

anchor.snippet: the zeta function of a quadratic field being the product of the Riemann zeta function

annotations.note: The Dedekind zeta function is defined (NumberField.dedekindZeta), but its factorization ζ_K = ζ(s)·L(s,χ) for quadratic fields is not formalized.

annotations.label: Jacobi symbol

anchor.snippet: The Jacobi symbol is a generalization of the Legendre symbol

annotations.note: jacobiSym a b is defined as the product of legendreSym over the prime factors of b, the standard Jacobi symbol.

annotations.label: Jacobi's reciprocity law

anchor.snippet: if both numbers are positive and odd

annotations.note: jacobiSym.quadratic_reciprocity gives J(a|b)=(-1)^(a/2·b/2)·J(b|a) for odd a,b.

annotations.label: Jacobi symbol caveat

anchor.snippet: it does not necessarily follow that the numerator is a quadratic residue of the denominator

annotations.note: The caveat is reflected by ZMod.isSquare_of_jacobiSym_eq_one requiring a prime modulus (the converse fails for composite b), but the counterexample itself is not stated.

annotations.label: Jacobi symbol example

anchor.snippet: 2 is a residue modulo the primes 7, 23 and 31

annotations.note: A specific numeric Jacobi-symbol illustration; not present as a statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Eisenstein's formula for Jacobi symbols

anchor.snippet: Eisenstein's formula requires relative primality conditions

annotations.note: Eisenstein's reciprocity formula for Jacobi symbols (odd coprime arguments) is jacobiSym.quadratic_reciprocity.

annotations.label: Hilbert symbol

anchor.snippet: where a and b are any two nonzero rational numbers

annotations.note: No Hilbert symbol is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hilbert reciprocity law

anchor.snippet: for fixed a and b and varying v , is 1 for all but finitely many v

annotations.note: Hilbert's reciprocity (product of local Hilbert symbols is 1) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadratic fields are subfields of cyclotomic fields

anchor.snippet: quadratic fields are subfields of cyclotomic fields

annotations.note: Special case of Kronecker–Weber; embedding every quadratic field into a cyclotomic field via Gauss sums is not a single named Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Quadratic character for Gaussian integers

anchor.snippet: For an odd Gaussian prime

annotations.note: Mathlib has GaussianInt arithmetic but no quadratic-residue character over ℤ[i].

annotations.label: Quadratic reciprocity in Gaussian integers

anchor.snippet: be distinct Gaussian primes where a and c are odd and b and d are even

annotations.note: Quadratic reciprocity for Gaussian primes is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadratic character for Eisenstein integers

anchor.snippet: For an Eisenstein prime

annotations.note: No quadratic character over the Eisenstein integers is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadratic reciprocity in Eisenstein integers

anchor.snippet: be distinct Eisenstein primes where a and c are not divisible by 3

annotations.note: Quadratic reciprocity for Eisenstein primes is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadratic character in imaginary quadratic fields

anchor.snippet: Let k be an imaginary quadratic number field with ring of integers

annotations.note: No quadratic-residue character in rings of integers of imaginary quadratic fields is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Herglotz's reciprocity law

anchor.snippet: Herglotz proved

annotations.note: Herglotz's reciprocity law for imaginary quadratic fields is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadratic character for polynomials over a finite field

anchor.snippet: Let F be a finite field with q = p n elements

annotations.note: No quadratic residue character for polynomials over a finite field is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dedekind's reciprocity for polynomials

anchor.snippet: Dedekind proved that if

annotations.note: Dedekind's quadratic reciprocity law for polynomials over 𝔽_q is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Artin reciprocity

anchor.snippet: a general theorem for which all known reciprocity laws are special cases

annotations.note: Artin reciprocity (the main theorem of class field theory) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiplicative group of ZMod p is cyclic

anchor.snippet: the multiplicative group of a finite field of order p is cyclic of order p-1

annotations.note: Cyclicity of the units of a finite integral domain (hence finite field) is the instance instIsCyclicUnitsOfFinite in Mathlib.RingTheory.IntegralDomain; order p−1 follows from ZMod.card.
